majors.notes: counted official UC Berkeley Catalog results after enabling the 'Major' filter on the Programs page; titles extracted from official page: https://undergraduate.catalog.berkeley.edu/programs

admissions.testing_policy: UC Berkeley follows the University of California first-year requirements page cited here, which lists A-G coursework and GPA requirements and does not require SAT/ACT scores on that page; this pass does not attach a Berkeley-only test-policy sentence.
admissions.gpa_policy: Students applying to Berkeley should meet minimum requirements including a 3.0 GPA in A-G courses taken in the 10th and 11th grades, but Berkeley warns that satisfying minimum requirements is often not enough to be competitive for selection.
admissions.course_rigor: Berkeley says it selects first-year students through a holistic review that considers weighted and unweighted UC GPA, planned 12th-grade courses, and the full record of achievement in college preparatory courses.
admissions.recommendations: unknown from the official Berkeley and UC pages used in this pass
admissions.essays: unknown from the official Berkeley and UC pages used in this pass

projects_research: Berkeley considers personal qualities including leadership ability, character, motivation, insight, tenacity, initiative, originality, intellectual independence, responsibility, maturity, and demonstrated concern for others and for the community.
Berkeley considers accomplishments in a field of intellectual or creative endeavor, extracurricular activities, leadership, employment, and volunteer service.

evidence.claim: Satisfying Berkeley's minimum requirements is often not enough to be competitive for selection.
evidence.source_excerpt: Since Berkeley is a competitive campus, satisfying the minimum requirements is often not enough to be competitive for selection.

evidence.claim: Berkeley uses a holistic review and considers the full record of achievement in college preparatory courses.
evidence.source_excerpt: Using a broad concept of merit ... the applicant's full record of achievement in college preparatory courses.

evidence.claim: Berkeley considers accomplishments in a field of intellectual or creative endeavor.; Berkeley considers accomplishments in extracurricular activities, leadership, employment, and volunteer service.
evidence.source_excerpt: accomplishments in a field of intellectual or creative endeavor; accomplishments in extracurricular activities ... leadership ... employment; and volunteer service.
With the official 'Major' filter selected, the Programs page reports 113 results found.

evidence.claim: UC Berkeley follows the University of California first-year requirements page cited here, which lists A-G coursework and GPA requirements and does not require SAT/ACT scores on that page; this pass does not attach a Berkeley-only test-policy sentence.
evidence.source_excerpt: The UC first-year requirements page lists A-G coursework and GPA requirements and does not list SAT/ACT as a required step on the page used in this pass.

evidence.claim: Students applying to Berkeley should meet minimum requirements including a 3.0 GPA in A-G courses taken in the 10th and 11th grades, but Berkeley warns that satisfying minimum requirements is often not enough to be competitive for selection.
evidence.source_excerpt: Students who apply to Berkeley should meet the following minimum requirements ... Have a 3.0 GPA in A-G courses taken in the 10th and 11th grades ... satisfying the minimum requirements is often not enough to be competitive for selection.

evidence.claim: Berkeley says it selects first-year students through a holistic review that considers weighted and unweighted UC GPA, planned 12th-grade courses, and the full record of achievement in college preparatory courses.
evidence.source_excerpt: The campus selects its first-year class through an assessment that includes a holistic review of your academic performance ... your weighted and unweighted UC GPA ... planned 12th grade courses ... full record of achievement in college preparatory courses.

evidence.claim: Berkeley considers personal qualities including leadership ability, character, motivation, insight, tenacity, initiative, originality, intellectual independence, responsibility, maturity, and demonstrated concern for others and for the community.; Berkeley considers accomplishments in a field of intellectual or creative endeavor, extracurricular activities, leadership, employment, and volunteer service.
evidence.source_excerpt: Personal qualities ... leadership ability ... initiative ... concern for others and for the community ... accomplishments in extracurricular activities ... leadership ... employment; and volunteer service.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 5 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of California Berkeley' (legacyId=1072) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

Aerospace Engineering; Aerospace Engineering and Business Administration; African American Studies; American Studies; Analytics; Ancient Greek and Roman Studies; Anthropology; Applied Mathematics; Architecture; Art; Asian American and Asian Diaspora Studies; Astrophysics

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (112 titles).
Phase 3 structured upgrade completed with improved majors counting, admissions extraction, and query-ready records.
Berkeley testing policy is still tied to the broader University of California requirements page rather than a Berkeley-only sentence captured in this pass.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.
School-people enrichment includes school-specific override data sourced from official university domains for populated sections.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: Selected from the official UC Berkeley EECS faculty directory and ranked by current campus-wide/public prominence reflected in official roles, named chairs, lab leadership, and breadth of research visibility on official Berkeley profiles.
popular_professors.source_summary: Extracted from official UC Berkeley EECS faculty directory and official Berkeley faculty profile pages on eecs.berkeley.edu / www2.eecs.berkeley.edu.

items.name: Jennifer Chayes
items.bio: Professor, Dean. Jennifer Chayes is Dean of the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society and a Berkeley professor of EECS, mathematics, statistics, and information.

items.name: Michael Jordan
items.bio: Professor Emeritus, Professor in the Graduate School. Michael I. Jordan is the Pehong Chen Distinguished Professor in EECS and Statistics whose research spans computational, statistical, biological, cognitive, and social sciences.

items.name: Stuart J. Russell
items.bio: Professor. Stuart Russell is a Berkeley EECS professor, holder of the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering, and director of the Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI).

items.name: Pieter Abbeel
items.bio: Professor. Pieter Abbeel directs the Berkeley Robot Learning Lab and co-directs the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab.

items.name: Jitendra Malik
items.bio: Professor. Jitendra Malik is the Arthur J. Chick Professor of EECS with appointments in vision science, cognitive science, and bioengineering.

items.name: Dawn Song
items.bio: Professor. Dawn Song is a Berkeley computer science professor and co-director of the Berkeley Center for Responsible Decentralized Intelligence with research in AI safety, security, privacy, and decentralized systems.

items.name: Ion Stoica
items.bio: Professor. Ion Stoica is a Berkeley EECS professor, holder of the Xu Bao Chancellor's Chair, director of SkyLab, and a researcher in cloud computing and AI systems.

items.name: David A. Patterson
items.bio: Professor Emeritus. David Patterson is the Pardee Professor of Computer Science, Emeritus, known for Berkeley leadership in computer architecture and systems research.

items.name: Ken Goldberg
items.department: Industrial Engineering and Operations Research / EECS
items.bio: Professor. Ken Goldberg is a Berkeley professor supervising research in robotics and automation, with secondary appointments in EECS, the School of Information, and Art Practice.

items.name: Anca Dragan
items.bio: Associate Professor. Anca Dragan runs the InterACT Lab, focusing on AI alignment and human-AI collaboration for agents and robots.

items.name: Alexei (Alyosha) Efros
items.bio: Professor in EECS with research interests in artificial intelligence and graphics, according to his official Berkeley faculty profile.

items.name: Anant Sahai
items.bio: Professor in EECS with research interests spanning control, machine learning, optimization, and signal processing on his official Berkeley faculty profile.

items.name: Kannan Ramchandran
items.bio: Professor in EECS with Berkeley-listed research interests in information, data, networks, communication sciences, and signal processing.

items.name: Joseph Gonzalez
items.bio: Associate Professor in EECS whose official Berkeley faculty profile highlights work in databases, AI, machine learning systems, and data systems.

items.name: Raluca Ada Popa
items.bio: Associate Professor in EECS with Berkeley-listed research interests in computer security, privacy, databases, and systems.

items.name: Sergey Levine
items.bio: Associate Professor in EECS with official Berkeley profile interests in artificial intelligence, robotics, and control.

items.name: Prabal Dutta
items.bio: Professor in EECS whose official Berkeley profile lists research in cyber-physical systems, power and energy, and hardware/software systems.

items.name: Alvin Cheung
items.bio: Professor in EECS with official Berkeley-listed interests in database management systems and programming systems.

items.name: John DeNero
items.bio: Teaching Professor in EECS with official Berkeley profile interests in artificial intelligence and education.

items.name: Sarah Chasins
items.bio: Assistant Professor in EECS with official Berkeley profile interests in programming systems and human-computer interaction.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Recent Berkeley alumni/professional-program graduates identified from official College of Chemistry alumni board pages and official PDP alumni spotlights; ranked by recency first, then specificity of current role.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official UC Berkeley College of Chemistry alumni profile pages and the official Product Development Program alumni spotlight page.

items.name: Nouf Al Hazmi
items.bio: Nouf graduated from UC Berkeley's MBPE program in 2024 and is Founder and CEO of Plastus Biotech in Berkeley.
items.description: Founder and CEO, Plastus Biotech
